title: DSDS Status Definition
description: The lifecycle status of a design system artifact and optional platform-specific readiness tracking. Used across all DSDS document types to indicate maturity and readiness.

status.description: The lifecycle status of the artifact. Standard values: 'draft' (under development, not ready for use), 'experimental' (available but API may change without notice), 'stable' (production-ready, changes follow semver), 'deprecated' (scheduled for removal). Custom values are permitted and SHOULD be lowercase kebab-case (e.g., 'sunset', 'archived', 'beta').

platformStatusEntry.description: The readiness status of an artifact on a specific platform. Tracks lifecycle stage, version availability, and optional deprecation notices independently per platform.

status.description: The lifecycle status of the artifact on this platform. Uses the same vocabulary as the top-level status: 'draft', 'experimental', 'stable', 'deprecated'. Custom values are permitted.

since.description: The version in which this artifact became available on this platform.

deprecationNotice.description: Required when status is 'deprecated'. Explains what to use instead on this platform.

description.description: Optional notes about the artifact's status on this platform (e.g., 'Available as a Web Component wrapper; native implementation planned for v4').

platformStatus.description: Platform-specific readiness or availability status for an artifact. Keys are platform identifiers and values describe the artifact's status on that platform. Platform keys are freeform strings — common values include 'react', 'web-component', 'ios', 'android', 'flutter', 'figma', 'sketch', and 'compose'. When present, the top-level `status` property represents the overall lifecycle stage of the artifact, while `platformStatus` provides granular per-platform detail. An artifact MAY be 'stable' overall while individual platforms are still 'experimental' or 'draft'.
